name: Canada
summary: English-speaking with a best-in-class 3-year citizenship route and 3-year master's PGWP, but a ~49% study-cap cut, ~36% national study-permit approval (Bangladesh among hardest hit), dormant STEM Express Entry draws and tightened spousal work permits create real friction for a two-engineer Bangladeshi couple.

links.title: IRCC immigrate to Canada (official)
IRCC study permit (official)

job-market.summary: Deep English-speaking demand for software/AI talent (~98% of IT depts planning transformation), but 2026 is selective hiring after 2023-24 layoffs.
job-market.reasoning: Toronto, Vancouver, Montreal and Waterloo host large tech sectors and Robert Half's 2026 guide keeps software/AI roles in the most-in-demand list with specialized talent (AI/ML, cybersecurity, cloud) scarce and paying a 20-30% premium. However, the market is in selective hiring after the 2023-24 layoff wave, so two newcomers without Canadian experience face real friction before landing roles; both engineers are hireable but placement is not immediate.
evidence: Robert Half 2026: software/AI engineers among top in-demand tech roles; ~98% of IT departments planning major digital transformation, keeping specialized talent scarce
2026 SWE pay: entry ~CAD 60-100k, mid ~CAD 100-130k, senior Toronto ~CAD 135-178k (city salary benchmarks)
Market in selective hiring post 2023-24 layoffs; AI/ML, cybersecurity, cloud and Python skills add 20-30% pay premium

links.title: Government of Canada Job Bank
Robert Half - 2026 IT & tech salary guide trends (Canada)

visa-access.summary: Biometrics in Dhaka but no SDS, ~35.7% national approval (Bangladesh among hardest hit), CAD 22,895 funds bar; medicals via New Delhi RMO.
visa-access.reasoning: Bangladeshis apply via the regular stream (SDS ended 8 Nov 2024) with biometrics at VFS Dhaka and IOM medicals locally, but the 2025 national study-permit approval rate fell to about 35.7% (refusals ~64%, toughest in a decade) and IRCC's Visa Integrity push hit India, Bangladesh and Nigeria hardest, with applications down ~40%. Proof-of-funds is CAD 22,895 per applicant (continuing into 2026) on top of tuition, financial insufficiency and weak documentation are top refusal reasons, and medical results route to IRCC's New Delhi Regional Medical Office, so the scrutiny and funds bar is heavy for this profile.
evidence: SDS closed 8 Nov 2024 - all Bangladeshi applicants use the regular stream
2025 national study-permit approval ~35.7% (refusal ~64%, toughest in a decade); India/Bangladesh/Nigeria hardest hit, applications down ~40% early 2025
Proof-of-funds CAD 22,895 + tuition per applicant (continuing into 2026); financial insufficiency and weak docs top refusal reasons; biometrics in Dhaka, medicals via New Delhi RMO

links.title: IRCC study permit (official)
Canada rejects nearly two in three study-permit applicants (The PIE News)

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after 1,095 days (3 of 5 yrs); only CLB 4 language; dual citizenship fully allowed; passport ranks 7th-8th (181-182 destinations).
citizenship.reasoning: Among the fastest routes globally for this couple: physically present 1,095 days in the 5 years before applying (min 730 as PR), a modest CLB 4 speaking/listening test for ages 18-54, and Canada is one of the most dual-citizenship-friendly nations so they need not renounce Bangladeshi nationality. The 2026 Henley index ranks the passport 7th-8th with visa-free access to 181-182 destinations; IRCC quotes ~13 months processing plus a 2-4 month oath wait, keeping the passport timeline realistic.
evidence: 1,095 days physical presence in 5 years (min 730 as PR); taxes filed for 3 years; CLB 4 (speak/listen) for ages 18-54
Dual citizenship permitted - no need to give up the Bangladeshi passport
Henley Passport Index 2026: Canada 7th-8th, ~181-182 visa-free/visa-on-arrival destinations; citizenship processing ~13 months + 2-4 month oath wait

links.title: IRCC Canadian citizenship (official)
Canada retains top-ten spot in 2026 Henley Passport Index

post-study-work.summary: Master's grads get a 3-year open PGWP regardless of program length, field-of-study exempt; CLB 7 language proof required (easy for IT).
post-study-work.reasoning: Graduates of a university master's program (min 8 months) receive an open PGWP of up to 3 years regardless of program length and are exempt from the field-of-study restriction, letting both engineers work openly and switch employers. Degree grads must show CLB 7 across all four skills (~IELTS 6.0), which is straightforward for IT professionals; the only friction is the strict 180-day post-graduation application window and the general policy-tightening backdrop.
evidence: Master's grads: up to 3-year open PGWP regardless of program length (min 8-month program)
Degree grads (bachelor's/master's/PhD) exempt from PGWP field-of-study list
CLB 7 in all four skills required for university grads (~IELTS 6.0); must apply within 180 days of graduation

links.title: IRCC Post-Graduation Work Permit (official)
PGWP Canada 2026: eligibility & new rules

spouse-family.summary: Both can accompany; spousal open work permit survives for a 16+ month master's, but 2025-26 rules narrowed it and refuse final-term spouses.
spouse-family.reasoning: From 21 Jan 2025 a spousal open work permit (SOWP) is limited to spouses of master's programs of at least 16 months, doctoral or listed professional programs, so a standard thesis MSc qualifies with no TEER threshold; an additional rule effective 4 Mar 2026 refuses SOWPs (even renewals) when the student is in their final academic term. In the work phase the principal must hold a skilled job (TEER 0/1) with 16+ months authorization remaining, so both can work but only under conditions, not unconditionally as on a points-based skilled visa - and the final-term cut-off is a fresh downside.
evidence: SOWP for students restricted to master's >=16 months / doctoral / listed professional programs (21 Jan 2025); no TEER threshold for full-time master's/doctoral students
Effective 4 Mar 2026: SOWP refused (even on renewal) if student is in their final academic term
Work-phase SOWP requires principal in a TEER 0/1 occupation with 16+ months authorization remaining

links.title: IRCC work permit for spouses of students (official)
Spouses of international students 2025-2026: who still qualifies for an open work permit

msc-study.summary: Abundant English MSc; coursework tuition ~CAD 10-40k/yr (eng. high end) though research MSc often funded; 24h/wk work; cap-exempt from 2026.
msc-study.reasoning: Broad English-taught master's catalogue at strong universities, with international tuition typically CAD 10,000-40,000/yr (engineering at the higher end), while thesis/research MSc commonly carry TA/RA stipends and awards (CGS-M, Vanier CAD 50k, Mitacs Accelerate CAD 10k). Master's and doctoral students are exempt from the study-permit cap from 1 Jan 2026, students may work off-campus up to 24 hours/week, and the spouse can accompany - though sticker tuition plus CAD 20-30k living costs and the funds bar keep this short of best-in-class.
evidence: Wide English-taught MSc catalogue; international tuition ~CAD 10,000-40,000/yr (engineering higher end); living costs ~CAD 20,000-30,000/yr
Research MSc often funded via TA/RA + CGS-M / Vanier (CAD 50k) / Mitacs Accelerate (CAD 10k) awards
Master's/doctoral students exempt from the study-permit cap from 1 Jan 2026; off-campus work 24 hrs/week

links.title: IRCC study in Canada (official)
What international students need to know about the 2026-2028 plan (CIC News)

pr-pathway.summary: Clear ~3.5-4yr study->PGWP->CEC route, but CEC CRS ~507-515, STEM draws dormant since Apr 2024 and now need 1yr experience.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Express Entry (CEC after 1 yr skilled Canadian work) is a predictable route taking roughly 3.5-4 years from study start with PR processing of ~6 months, but 2026 CEC cut-offs sit at a high 507-515 with draw sizes stuck near 2,000 ITAs, and dedicated STEM category draws have not run since April 2024. From 18 Feb 2026 the STEM category minimum experience rose from 6 months to 1 year, and French at NCLC 7 unlocks far lower cut-offs (~409), so PR is attainable but competitive without French or a provincial (OINP/BC PNP Tech) nomination.
evidence: Study->PGWP->CEC pathway ~3.5-4 years; PR target held at 380k/yr 2026-2028, economic share ~64% (~239,800 in 2026)
2026 CEC CRS cut-offs ~507-515 (April draw 515); STEM category draws dormant since April 2024
STEM category min experience raised 6mo->1yr (18 Feb 2026); French draws cut to ~409; OINP / BC PNP Tech active alternatives

links.title: IRCC Express Entry (official)
Express Entry latest draw tracker (Moving2Canada)

income-cost.summary: Strong dual tech salaries (mid SWE ~CAD 100-130k) and softening rents (12 months down), but big-metro housing still erodes savings.
income-cost.reasoning: 2026 salary benchmarks put mid-level software engineers around CAD 100,000-130,000 and seniors higher, so a two-engineer household earns well. Toronto 1-bed rents eased to roughly CAD 2,100-2,340/month and Metro Vancouver ~CAD 2,100-2,500 (national rents down ~12 months straight), so dual income yields good savings, but high-metro housing affordability plus tax keep it short of exceptional.
evidence: 2026 SWE pay: mid ~CAD 100-130k, senior Toronto ~CAD 135-178k; dual income materially lifts household savings
Toronto 1-bed rent ~CAD 2,100-2,340/month; Metro Vancouver ~CAD 2,100-2,495 (down ~CAD 182 since Jan 2025)
National rents down ~12 consecutive months, easing the affordability crunch from its peak

links.title: Robert Half 2026 IT & tech salary guide (Canada)
Statistics Canada

healthcare.summary: Universal and free for PR/citizens, high quality, but ~28.6-week median waits and a family-doctor shortage.
healthcare.reasoning: Provincial plans cover permanent residents and citizens (waiting period capped at 3 months, often waived) at no point-of-care cost and quality is high. However the Fraser Institute's 2025 study put the median wait from GP referral to treatment at 28.6 weeks (second-longest on record, down from 30.0 in 2024; Ontario shortest at 19.2 weeks), millions lack a family doctor, and international students must buy provincial/private student insurance during study.
evidence: Universal provincial coverage for PR/citizens; <=3-month wait period, often waived
Median treatment wait 28.6 weeks in 2025 (Fraser Institute, down from 30.0 in 2024); Ontario shortest at 19.2 weeks, New Brunswick longest 60.9
Family-doctor shortage; international students need student health insurance during study

links.title: Government of Canada - access universal health care
Fraser Institute - Waiting Your Turn 2025

culture-language.summary: English daily and official multiculturalism; no language barrier for PR (CLB 7) or citizenship (CLB 4) outside Quebec.
culture-language.reasoning: English (and French in Quebec) is the working language, Canada has an official multiculturalism policy and one of the largest immigrant populations in the OECD, and PR/citizenship language thresholds (CLB 7 / CLB 4) are easily met by IT professionals - so day-to-day integration is straightforward. Only Quebec adds a French dimension, which this couple can avoid by settling in Ontario/BC; cold winters are the main livability caveat.
evidence: English working language outside Quebec; official multiculturalism policy
Large immigrant share of population (South Asians ~19% of the GTA) eases integration
No additional language barrier for English-speaking PR (CLB 7) / citizenship (CLB 4) applicants; harsh winters the main livability caveat

links.title: Government of Canada multiculturalism
IRCC citizenship language proof (CLB requirement)

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law (WJP 13th, 0.79) and peaceful (GPI 14th, 1.491); strong Charter protections for immigrants.
safety-law.reasoning: Canada ranks 13th of 143 on the 2025 WJP Rule of Law Index (score 0.79, down one spot from 12th) and tied 14th of 163 on the 2025 Global Peace Index (1.491), with Charter rights and broadly safe cities. The GPI score edged down from 1.449 in 2024 (a five-spot drop) on rising violent crime, slightly tempering an otherwise strong score; treatment of immigrants under law remains fair.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: 13th of 143 (score 0.79), down from 12th
Global Peace Index 2025: tied 14th of 163 (1.491, down from 1.449 in 2024, -5 spots) on rising violent crime
Charter rights and strong legal protections for immigrants

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 - global press release
WJP Rule of Law Index - Canada insights

politics.summary: Stable democracy but sharp 2025-28 immigration cuts (study -49%, temp residents -43%) and rising negative public sentiment add volatility.
politics.reasoning: Institutions are stable, but the Carney government's 2026-2028 plan holds PR at 380,000, cut new study-permit intake ~49% to 155,000, reduced total work permits ~37% to 230,000 and temporary residents ~43% (673,650 in 2025 to 385,000 in 2026) amid persistently negative public opinion on immigration driven by housing and economic strain. Master's/doctoral students are exempted from the study cap from 1 Jan 2026, which softens the impact for this couple, but the overall policy direction is clearly and volatilely tightening.
evidence: 2026-2028 plan: PR target 380k/yr; new study permits cut ~49% to 155k; work permits cut ~37% to 230k; temporary residents 673,650 (2025) -> 385,000 (2026), -43%
Public opinion on immigration remains negative (Abacus 2026); housing crisis and US tariff strain cited as drivers
Master's/doctoral students exempt from the study-permit cap (1 Jan 2026)

links.title: IRCC Supplementary Information 2026-2028 Immigration Levels Plan (official)
Canadians' views on immigration remain largely unchanged and overly negative (Abacus Data)

tax.summary: Moderate burden: OECD single-worker tax wedge 32.0% (26th lowest of 38); ~43% marginal at CAD 100k in Ontario.
tax.reasoning: The OECD single-worker tax wedge is 32.0% - the 26th lowest of 38 OECD members - so the effective burden on tech salaries is moderate rather than punishing. The combined federal-provincial marginal rate is about 43% at CAD 100k in Ontario (top combined ~53.5%), and the lowest federal personal rate dropped to 14% (effective 1 Jul 2025, 14.5% full-year 2025), modestly improving take-home.
evidence: OECD Taxing Wages: Canada single-worker tax wedge 32.0% (2024) - 26th lowest of 38 OECD members
Ontario combined marginal rate ~43% at CAD 100k; top combined ~53.5%
Lowest federal PIT rate cut 15%->14% (effective 1 Jul 2025; 14.5% full-year 2025)

links.title: OECD Taxing Wages 2026 - Canada
TaxTips.ca - combined federal & Ontario tax rates

muslim-diaspora.summary: Large Muslim (~1.78M) and Bangladeshi (~75k, ~26.6k in Toronto) communities and ubiquitous halal, but G7-highest deadly Islamophobic attacks.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Canada has ~1.78M Muslims (4.9%, 2021 census, South Asians the largest group at 37.6%) concentrated in the GTA, and ~75,245 single-origin Bangladeshi Canadians (~26,650 in the City of Toronto), so halal food, mosques and a familiar South-Asian community (19% of the GTA) are readily available. The trend is the caveat: the National Council of Canadian Muslims calls Canada the G7 country with the highest number of deadly Islamophobic attacks, with documented vandalism, threats and assaults, prompting a federal anti-Islamophobia guide in March 2025.
evidence: ~1.78M Muslims in Canada (4.9%, 2021 census); South Asians 37.6% of Muslims; South Asians ~19% of the GTA
~75,245 single-origin Bangladeshi Canadians (2021); ~26,650 in the City of Toronto; ~84% of Bangladeshi Canadians are Muslim
NCCM: Canada the G7 country with the most deadly Islamophobic attacks; first federal anti-Islamophobia guide issued March 2025 - rising hostility is the main risk
